chapter_title: Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
(a) An applicant for a permit under Section 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) , other than a permit under that section to operate a vehicle loaded with timber or pulp wood, wood chips, cotton, or agricultural products in their natural state, and an applicant for a permit under Section 623.321 (Permit) shall file with the department:

(1) a blanket bond; or

(2) an irrevocable letter of credit issued by a financial institution the deposits of which are guaranteed by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation.

(b) The bond or letter of credit must:

(1) be in the amount of $15,000 payable to the counties of this state;

(2) be conditioned that the applicant will pay a county for any damage to a road or bridge of the county caused by the operation of the vehicle:

(A) for which the permit is issued at a heavier weight than the maximum weights authorized by Subchapter B (Maximum Weight of Vehicle or Combination) of Chapter 621 (General Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size and Weight) or Section 621.301 (County’s Authority to Set Maximum Weights) or 623.321 (Permit) ; or

(B) that is in violation of Section 623.323 (Notification) ; and

(3) provide that the issuer is to notify the county and the applicant in writing promptly after a payment is made by the issuer on the bond or letter of credit.

(c) If an issuer of a bond or letter of credit pays under the bond or letter of credit, the permit holder shall file with the department before the 31st day after the date on which the payment is made:

(1) a replacement bond or letter of credit in the amount prescribed by Subsection (b) for the original bond or letter of credit; or

(2) a notification from the issuer of the existing bond or letter of credit that the bond or letter of credit has been restored to the amount prescribed by Subsection (b).

(d) If the filing is not made as required by Subsection (c), each permit held by the permit holder under Section 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) automatically expires on the 31st day after the date on which the payment is made on the bond or letter of credit.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 623. Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 623.012 — Security for Permit

(a) An applicant for a permit under Section 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) , other than a permit under that section to operate a vehicle loaded with timber or pulp wood, wood chips, cotton, or agricultural products in their natural state, and an applicant for a permit under Section 623.321 (Permit) shall file with the department:

    (1) a blanket bond; or

    (2) an irrevocable letter of credit issued by a financial institution the deposits of which are guaranteed by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation.

(b) The bond or letter of credit must:

    (1) be in the amount of $15,000 payable to the counties of this state;

    (2) be conditioned that the applicant will pay a county for any damage to a road or bridge of the county caused by the operation of the vehicle:

        (A) for which the permit is issued at a heavier weight than the maximum weights authorized by Subchapter B (Maximum Weight of Vehicle or Combination) of Chapter 621 (General Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size and Weight) or Section 621.301 (County’s Authority to Set Maximum Weights) or 623.321 (Permit) ; or

        (B) that is in violation of Section 623.323 (Notification) ; and

    (3) provide that the issuer is to notify the county and the applicant in writing promptly after a payment is made by the issuer on the bond or letter of credit.

(c) If an issuer of a bond or letter of credit pays under the bond or letter of credit, the permit holder shall file with the department before the 31st day after the date on which the payment is made:

    (1) a replacement bond or letter of credit in the amount prescribed by Subsection (b) for the original bond or letter of credit; or

    (2) a notification from the issuer of the existing bond or letter of credit that the bond or letter of credit has been restored to the amount prescribed by Subsection (b).

(d) If the filing is not made as required by Subsection (c), each permit held by the permit holder under Section 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) automatically expires on the 31st day after the date on which the payment is made on the bond or letter of credit.

*Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 410 (S.B.1814), Sec. 3, eff. September 1, 2021.*

provide that the issuer is to notify the county and the applicant in writing promptly after a payment is made by the issuer on the bond or letter of credit.

a replacement bond or letter of credit in the amount prescribed by Subsection (b) for the original bond or letter of credit; or
a notification from the issuer of the existing bond or letter of credit that the bond or letter of credit has been restored to the amount prescribed by Subsection (b).

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: If the filing is not made as required by Subsection (c), each permit held by the permit holder under Section 623.011 (Permit for Excess Axle or Gross Weight) automatically expires on the 31st day after the date on which the payment is made on the bond or letter of credit.
